rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF RICHARD PATUREL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/24/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The Notice of Hearing (proof of service of which was filed on 7/20/22) did not show the complete caption of the amended second account petition. Attorney costs already paid ($3,698.67) and reflected under "Legal Fees" on page 21 of the accounting and additional costs incurred from 8/28/20 to 3/16/21 have not yet been itemized (i.e., fully described) to show WHAT was filed or sent by Federal Express or via Rescue Delivery, etc. Invoice numbers alone are not adequate. (Why was it necessary to incur so many FedEx charges? Can documents not be mailed?) The attorney charged interest on overdue balances ($85.80 on 5/31/19 and $34.57 on 8/6/19). An appearance will likely be required for the Court to determine if these charges were reasonable, especially in light of the numerous FedEx or other courier charges, which might not have been necessary. (The bank statements filed in the confidential file on 7/20/22 were not originals and the conservator's signature on his declaration was not original.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/14/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: RICHARD PATUREL REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/24/22. No response to issues posted on 7/1/22 as the preliminary tentative ruling: Upon closer review of the related conservatorship accounting transfers to this trust, the "fiduciary acquisition value" of several of the "Assets Listed in Inventory" (there was no inventory) differ from the values shown in the conservatorship accounting. (See, for example, Carrier Global, Essential Utilities, Honeywell, and at least four others). It appears that the trust accounting will need to be amended. The "Miscellaneous Personal Property" is not miscellaneous at all: five very specific paintings make up the $12,500 in tangible personal property. Those five paintings were still shown in the conservatorship accounting as being on hand as of 12/31/21. There were five payments on 4/30/21 to Home Care Assistance: three for $7,392.00 and two for $7,436.00. Trustee's supplement filed on 6/24/22 addresses only four. The First Republic Bank statement for the period 8/11/21 - 9/10/21 (Exhibit D to the supplement filed on 6/24/22, not Exhibit C) shows $15,016.96 at the beginning and $95,559.05 at the end. The accounting shows $115,016.96 as of 8/31/21. Still no reconciliation is shown. [LRSF 14.34 D.] Exhibit A to the trustee's supplement filed on 6/24/22 (list of Federal Express bills) is not an adequate itemization of attorney costs. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 10/14/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MARIA ISABEL QUINTANILLA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/19/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1. The proof of payment of assessment fee must be filed before the most recent accounting will be reviewed and there may be other issues when the account is reviewed in full. 2. Please explain the staring balance of $8,710.91 in account #6096 - 457 (b) Pre-tax plan. The account alleges the beginning balance is $8,710.91. The 06/09/2021 Amended Partial No.:1/Final I&A alleges account #6069 has $8,710.91 in assets. The 06/29/2021 Final I&A alleges account #6069 has $9,866.45 in assets. The 10/20/2021 Final I&A alleges account #6069 has $8,709.41 in assets. The account statement ends 3/31/2021 alleging $8,709.41 in assets and begins with $9,866.45 in assets on 01/01/2021. With the withdrawal and administrative charges on the statement it appears there is $8,663.45 in assets at the beginning of the account and the account appears to need to be amended. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/12/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KANTABEN IL PATEL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/26/2022 again for same issues. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please explain the relationship of Manilal Patel to Decedent. 2) Please explain the relationship of Jay Patel to Decedent. Jay Patel is listed as a co-tenant and son of Petitioner on notice but is not listed on the petition page 2 under #9. 3) Please explain the legal basis for the court transferring trust assets by a spousal property petition. 4) Please file a copy of the Ishverbhai N. Patel Living Trust dated 12/15/1999 and any and all amendments. 5) Please rebut the presumption in evidence code 662 for the real property held in a trust. The owner of the legal title to property is presumed to be the owner of the full beneficial title. This presumption may be rebutted only by clear and convincing proof. (Evidence Code 662). 6) Please rebut the presumption in evidence code 662 for the real property held in the LLC. The owner of the legal title to property is presumed to be the owner of the full beneficial title. This presumption may be rebutted only by clear and convincing proof. (Evidence Code 662). 7) Please rebut the presumption in evidence code 662 for all 3 bank accounts. The owner of the legal title to property is presumed to be the owner of the full beneficial title. This presumption may be rebutted only by clear and convincing proof. (Evidence Code 662). 8) Please explain the legal basis for the court determining the Bank of America account ending in #6701 is community property when there are three names listed on the account Ishverbhai N Patel, Jayeshkumar I Patel, and Kantaven I Patel. 9) Please file a June 2022 or July 2022 account statement for Bank of America account ending in #6701. 10) Please explain the legal basis for the court determining the Freemont Bank account ending in #9260 is community property when the only names on the account are Jayeshkumar Patel and Ishverbhai N. Patel. 11) Please file a June 2022 or July 2022 account statement for Freemont Bank account ending in #9260. 12) Please explain the legal basis for the court determining the Citibank account ending in #5083 is community property when the only names on the account are I N Patel or J I Patel. 13) Please file a July 2022 account statement for Citibank account ending in #5083. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/12/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF OPAL DEAN KITCHIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/17/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The second amended petition, while file stamped 6/21/22, was not actually e-filed (made available for viewing) until 6/27/22 because of the paucity in clerk staffing. Mailing notice before the filing (e-filing) of the pleading requiring notice is not sufficient notice. The "Amended" Notice of Petition and "Amended" proof of publication are not correct notice. Notice of a SECOND AMENDED PETITION, etc., should have been mailed and published. If a child survived, nieces and nephews are not heirs or interested parties in an intestate estate. (If the petitioner is the nephew of the sole heir's mother, he is not necessarily the decedent's nephew or greatnephew.) What was the predeceased son's date of death? [LRSF 14.18 C. and D.] Notice of the second amended petition must be mailed and published, showing the hearing date of 10/17/22. The petitioner's signed and verified SUPPLEMENT to the second amended petition must be filed to disclose the date of death of the decedent's predeceased son. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/7/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THOMAS RUTHERFORD JACKSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/14/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 2. A proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect California creditors, even if the will waives, or all beneficiaries waive, bond. A declaration or attachment to the petition setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000. (LRSF 14.16 F.) 3. Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed with a cover page, separately, and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] 4. Petitioner must file and give notice on the mandatory judicial council form DE-121 that complies with Local Rule 14.1. D. 5. Page 4 of the petition under #8 does not state the age of the persons listed. The Decedent's parents and their date of death are not listed. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/6/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CAROL BARBARA HICKS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/17/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] No courtesy copies. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] Notwithstanding the lack of courtesy copies, the petition was reviewed online as a ONE-TIME courtesy in hopes of avoiding further continuances later. The petitioner (the person named in item 2 of the petition) is missing from item 8 of the petition. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? Proof of publication has not been filed. Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities has not been filed. The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) has not been filed. Form DE-147S must be filed separately with a cover sheet, conventionally, and confidentially. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/7/22. (The original will was just lodged on 8/15/22 and has not yet been delivered to the assigned examiner as of 8/17/22.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
